latest.content:
Today 11:46
RNS Number : 4022K
R8 Capital Investments PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
R8 CAPITAL INVESTMENTS PLC
("R8" or the "Company")
Annual Financial Report to 31 December 2025
The Company announces that its annual report and accounts for the period ending 31 December 2025 have been published on the Company's website
https://www.r8plc.com/investors/results-reports-presentations
and have been submitted and are available for inspection at the National Storage Mechanism
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
.
Further to the Company's announcement of 1 May 2026 regarding the temporary suspension of the listing and trading in the Company's ordinary shares, and following the publication of its audited Annual Report and Accounts for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, the Company will apply to the Financial Conduct Authority for the restoration of the listing of its ordinary shares and will seek the resumption of trading in its ordinary shares on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
Further announcements will follow shortly.
- ENDS -

latest.title: Suspension of Listing and Proposed Fundraising
latest.content:
Today 13:48
RNS Number : 8554C
R8 Capital Investments PLC
01 May 2026
1 May 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Suspension of Listing
and
Proposed Fundraising
FY25 Results and Suspension of Listing
Further to its announcement of 28 April 2026, the Company announces that as of 7:30am today, its securities of ordinary shares of £0.01 each have been temporarily suspended from listing in the Equity Shares (Shell Companies) Category of the Official List of the FCA and trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The Company expects to publish its FY25 Results by the end of June and will make a further application to the FCA to lift the suspension on trading in its securities once the FY25 Results have been published.
The defined terms in the announcement of 28 April 2026 shall have the same meanings when used in this announcement.
Proposed Fundraising
The Company is pleased to announce it has entered into non-binding, conditional heads of terms for the proposed subscription by certain investors for a minimum of £500,000 in aggregate principal value for the issue of convertible loan notes by the Company terms to be agreed. The subscription is subject to, amongst other things, satisfactory KYC and AML being provided by the proposed investors. The Company anticipates completion of the subscription by 11 May 2026 and will accordingly update the market in due course.
Enquiries
Company
Jonathan Rowland / Richard Morecroft

Corporate Broker

latest.title: Audited Accounts to 31 December 2025 & Suspension
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 12:56
RNS Number : 2543C
R8 Capital Investments PLC
28 April 2026
28 April 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Publication of the Annual Financial Report
for the year ended 31 December 2025
and
Temporary Suspension of Listing
R8 Capital Investments plc provides the following update regarding the publication of its annual financial report for the year ended 31 December 2025 ("
FY25 Results
").
The Company will not be in a position to publish its FY25 Results by close of business on 30 April 2026, being the latest date permitted for publication of the FY25 Results under DTR 4.1.3R.
The unforeseen delay to the audit is principally due to the ongoing wind-down of its Fibermode subsidiary which has extended the audit process.
The Company is working together with its auditors to finalise the audit and will provide a further update in due course. The revised date for publication of FY25 Results is a result of administrative delay only and does not indicate any irregularities in the FY25 Results.
The Company will therefore apply to the Financial Conduct Authority ("
FCA
") for the listing on the Official List of the FCA of its ordinary shares of £0.01 each to be temporarily suspended with effect from 7.30 am on 1 May 2026 until publication of the FY25 Results.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for the content of this announcement.

latest.content:
25 Mar 2026 12:21
RNS Number : 1163Y
R8 Capital Investments PLC
25 March 2026
25 March 2026
R8 Capital Investments Plc
(the "
Company
")
Admission of Shares
Further to the Company's announcement on 27 June 2025, an application for Admission of the
2,619,782 ordinary
shares of £0.01 each in the share capital of the Company ("
Shares
")
to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange will be made and it is expected that admission of the Shares will become effective on or around 2 April 2026 and that dealings in the Shares will commence at that time.
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital comprises 107,411,062 ordinary shares of £0.01 each. The above figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: MODE.L is a small‑cap technology firm that builds and licenses cloud‑based analytics tools for mid‑market enterprises.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Its solutions sit at the intersection of big data and automation, a space that is expanding as companies look to cut costs and improve decision‑making. The company’s platform is used by a handful of regional businesses, giving it a foothold that could grow if it captures more customers.
company_30s.current_state: After a period of suspension, MODE.L has been relisted and has issued new shares to VVV Sports Ltd. The company’s financial reports are sparse and it has not yet shown a clear path to profitability, but the relisting and share issuance suggest it is trying to shore up capital and stabilize operations.

rns_narrative: The company has moved from a suspended status back to the market, indicating regulatory compliance and a willingness to engage investors again.
It has issued new shares, which may dilute existing holdings but also inject fresh cash into the business.
Recent interim results were unaudited, hinting at limited resources for full‑scale financial reporting.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope – investors are waiting for a clear signal before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some chatter suggests the stock will rally once positive news surfaces, with traders hoping a relisting‑related announcement could trigger a spike.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others caution that the new share issue could erode value and that the company’s lack of audited results makes its fundamentals murky.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from neutral to tentative optimism as the relisting was announced, but the community remains largely silent, awaiting more concrete data.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent activity, meaning the market is currently unaware of the company’s situation.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report (scheduled for early Q2) could provide the first audited snapshot of the company’s cash position and revenue trajectory.
A potential partnership or customer win announced by the management team would demonstrate product traction and could lift sentiment.
Volume spikes at the 52‑week low or a breakout above a recent resistance level would signal renewed buying interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on daily trading volume – a sudden uptick paired with a price move above the previous high would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis. Also monitor any statements about cost‑cutting or revenue growth from the board.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery after a deep drop. The APEX score indicates tight compression, suggesting that a breakout could be imminent if a catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: MODE.L has experienced a deep crash, which historically produces outsized rebounds. If the stock recovers to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, the upside could be 150–200% from the current price. The relisting and new share issuance provide a structural reset that could attract institutional buyers.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The lack of audited results and the dilution from the new shares increase downside risk. If the company fails to show any revenue growth or cost control, the price could fall back to the 52‑week low or below, eroding the upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the depth of the current decline (roughly 70–80% from the high). Potential gain, if the mean reversion plays out, could be 3–4 times that loss, giving a 1:3 to 1:4 risk‑reward profile.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the recent 20‑day moving average with volume exceeding 150% of the 20‑day average. Allocate 10–15% of your portfolio to the position, and set a stop just below the 50‑day moving average to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of at least 200% that accompanies a move past the 52‑week low. A clear bullish candle on a 4‑hour chart would be a good trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a delisting, bankruptcy filing, or a material fraud claim, exit immediately. No other terminal risks are identified at this time.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MODE.L’s deep crash, combined with a fresh relisting and share issuance, creates a high‑asymmetry contrarian play that could deliver significant upside if a clear catalyst triggers a mean‑reversion rally.
